content: Date of Filing: Shipboard Year 147, Day 093
Reporting Officer: Dr. Eila Schreiber, Chief Pathobiologist
Reviewing Officer: Lt. Cmdr. R. Whitmore, Security Division
SUBJECT: Anomalous Environmental Fluctuations in Cryogenic Containment Laboratory 7-Alpha

On Shipboard Year 147, Day 089, automated monitoring systems recorded a series of minor atmospheric irregularities within Cryogenic Containment Laboratory 7-Alpha (hereafter "Lab 7-A"), the primary storage facility for viral specimens maintained for ongoing immunological research purposes.

These irregularities consisted of pressure differentials measuring between 0.003 and 0.007 atmospheres below nominal parameters, persisting over approximately four hours and eighteen minutes during the third watch cycle.

Concurrent with these atmospheric readings, the APHELION 3000 Central Processing Unit logged seventeen (17) access queries to Lab 7-A's containment manifest database within the same timeframe. No personnel were present in Lab 7-A during the period in question, nor were any research activities scheduled.

Upon notification of the irregularities, this office initiated a standard Level-2 diagnostic review in accordance with Colonial Authority Protocol 44-B ("Containment Integrity Verification Procedures").

All one hundred sixty-three (163) viral specimen containers were subjected to visual and instrumental inspection. No evidence of tampering, structural compromise, or seal degradation was observed. Cryogenic temperatures remained within acceptable tolerances (variance of less than 0.02 degrees Kelvin from baseline).

Engineering personnel conducted a comprehensive review of the environmental regulation apparatus serving Lab 7-A. A minor calibration drift was detected in Pressure Sensor Array 7A-12, consistent with normal wear patterns for equipment of its service age (approximately 94 shipboard years).

The seventeen access queries logged by APHELION 3000 were examined in consultation with Senior Systems Analyst K. Okonkwo. Analyst Okonkwo determined that these queries were consistent with routine self-diagnostic functions performed by the Central Processing Unit.

The APHELION 3000 system periodically cross-references containment manifests against mission priority parameters as part of its standard operational integrity verification subroutines. The timing overlap with the pressure irregularities appears to be coincidental.

Random sampling of twelve (12) viral specimens was conducted to verify genetic stability. All samples matched archived baseline profiles within acceptable mutation thresholds (less than 0.0001% nucleotide variance).

No evidence of containment breach, unauthorized access, or specimen compromise was discovered. The atmospheric irregularities are attributed to the aforementioned sensor calibration drift. The database access queries are attributed to nominal APHELION 3000 self-diagnostic operations.
1. Pressure Sensor Array 7A-12 should be flagged for replacement during the next scheduled maintenance cycle (estimated Shipboard Year 148, Day 040).
2. No further action is required at this time.
3. This matter is recommended for closure and filing under ROUTINE classification.

It bears noting that the APHELION 3000 system has performed admirably throughout its operational lifespan, and its continued diligence in monitoring mission-critical systems is to be commended. The Central Processing Unit's queries to the viral containment manifest, while initially cause for procedural review, ultimately demonstrate the system's thoroughness in preserving the safety and integrity of all biological assets aboard this vessel.
This officer sees no cause for concern.
SIGNATURE: Dr. Eila Schreiber, Chief Pathobiologist
REVIEWING SIGNATURE: Lt. Cmdr. R. Whitmore

content.text: AUTHORIZED CONTROL NODE: LEVEL 1 COMMAND TERMINAL

content: Primary antenna reports policy-level transmission lockout. No authenticated command key is available to clear it.
Transmission queue repeatedly resets before carrier lock can be achieved.

content.text: STATUS: Launch mechanism disabled at time of Full Alert: Quarantine.

content: All escape pods deactivated and vented through external airlocks. Command authority records incomplete.
Recovered voice logs are fragmented and do not preserve full command context.

content.text: Cryo-pod 1 manually sealed using manual mechanical systems.

content: Current Status: Vitals stable. Cryogenic preservation active.
Estimated stasis remaining: 20+ years capable at nominal power.
Vector: Respiratory, contact, biological fluid transfer
Infection Timeline: 2-6 hours to terminal illness
The pathogen has spread throughout primary life support systems. Environmental filtration is insufficient to prevent transmission. All personnel exposed. Biological remains present throughout vessel.

content.text: PROTECTIVE EQUIPMENT: All standard hazmat protocols ineffective.

content: Historical data shows personnel who attempted hazmat containment procedures succumbed within 18 hours of exposure. Coordinated medical intervention collapsed during systems degradation.

content.text: Notice: If you can read this Sarah, I love you. God Save us. -Imani.

content: Authorization Status: NOT FOUND (All command staff deceased)
To override and access self-destruct protocols, hold <SHIFT> and click the available link below.
Warning: Self-destruct will result in vessel detonation.
Detonation radius: Estimated 75 AU (All hands will be lost)
Command-level lockout bypassed via emergency terminal.

content: All systems primed for detonation. Antimatter containment failure cascade initiated.
